title: Interactive downstream analysis with ShinyNGS
code: library(devtools) install_github('pinin4fjords/shinyngs')

title: Interactive downstream analysis with ShinyNGS
text: data structures used Shinyngs build SummarizedExperiment. One SummarizedExperiment can multiple ‘assays’, essentially matrices samples columns ‘features’ (transcripts genes) rows, representing different results features samples. handy compare results processing, example. ExploratorySummarizedExperiment extends SummarizedExperiment include slots relating annotation, associated results ‘tests’, providing p values q values. ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList container one ExploratorySummarizedExperiment objects, intented describe overall study, .e. one experiments set samples, different sets features experiment. ExploratorySummarizedExperimentListList therefore used supply study-wide things contrasts, gene sets, url roots creating links etc.

what: Simple example working from a SummarizedExperiment
title: Interactive downstream analysis with ShinyNGS
text: see quickly build RNA-seq app simple SummarizedExperiment, can use example data airway package. just convert RangedSummarizedExperiment ExploratorySummarizedExperiment, add list objects, represent study. build run app. example, basic app just heatmaps: Note use prepareApp generate proper ui server, passed Shiny. can build comprehensive app multiple panels aimed RNA-seq: Airway provides info dataset, can add object build app: app knows gene IDs, however, aren’t informative gene expression plots etc. can add row metadata fix :
code: library(shinyngs)  data(airway, package = 'airway') ese <- as(airway, 'ExploratorySummarizedExperiment') eselist <- ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList(ese) app <- prepareApp('heatmap', eselist) shiny::shinyApp(ui = app$ui, server = app$server) app <- prepareApp('rnaseq', eselist) shiny::shinyApp(ui = app$ui, server = app$server) data(airway, package = 'airway') expinfo <- metadata(airway)[[1]]  eselist <- ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList(   ese,   title = expinfo@title,   author = expinfo@name,   description = abstract(expinfo) ) app <- prepareApp('rnaseq', eselist) shiny::shinyApp(ui = app$ui, server = app$server) # Use Biomart to retrieve some annotation, and add it to the object  library(biomaRt) attributes <- c(   'ensembl_gene_id', # The sort of ID your results are keyed by   'entrezgene', # Will be used mostly for gene set based stuff   'external_gene_name' # Used to annotate gene names on the plot )  mart <- useMart(biomart = 'ENSEMBL_MART_ENSEMBL', dataset = 'hsapiens_gene_ensembl', host='www.ensembl.org') annotation <- getBM(attributes = attributes, mart = mart) annotation <- annotation[order(annotation$entrezgene),]  mcols(ese) <- annotation[match(rownames(ese), annotation$ensembl_gene_id),]  # Tell shinyngs what the ids are, and what field to use as a label  ese@idfield <- 'ensembl_gene_id' ese@labelfield <- 'external_gene_name'  # Re-build the app  eselist <- ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList(   ese,   title = expinfo@title,   author = expinfo@name,   description = abstract(expinfo) ) app <- prepareApp('rnaseq', eselist) shiny::shinyApp(ui = app$ui, server = app$server)

what: More complex use case: the zhangneurons Example dataset
title: Interactive downstream analysis with ShinyNGS
text: airway fine, contains information differential expression. shinyngs provides extra slots differential analyses, among things. example ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList based Zhang et al study neurons glia (http://www.jneurosci.org/content/34/36/11929.long) included zhangneurons package, can used demonstrate available features. dataset includes transcript- gene- level quantification estimates (ExporatorySummarizedExperiments within ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList, three levels processing (raw, filtered, normalised) assays slots . Note: data generated using Salmon (https://combine-lab.github.io/salmon/) quantification, results may therefore slightly different authors’ online tool (use Salmon). Install data package: … load data. data can used build application: example generates full application designed RNA-seq analysis. Remember individual components can created :
code: library(devtools) install_github('pinin4fjords/zhangneurons') library(shinyngs) data("zhangneurons") app <- prepareApp("rnaseq", zhangneurons) shiny::shinyApp(app$ui, app$server) app <- prepareApp("heatmap", zhangneurons) shiny::shinyApp(app$ui, app$server)

title: Interactive downstream analysis with ShinyNGS
text: shinyngs build number components built using Shiny’s module framework, many used multiple times complex applications one described RNA-seq. Included modules currently: heatmap - provides controls display making heat maps based user criteria. pca - provides controls display interactive PCA plot. boxplot - provides controls display interactive boxplot. dendro - clustering samples dendrogram plotted ggdendro}. gene - bar plot showing gene expression table fold changes etc (appropriate) simpletable - simple display using datatables (via DT package) show table download button. complex table displays (controls, example) can build module. assaydatatable - shows assaydata() content selected experiment. selectmatrix - provides controls output subsetting profided assay data prior plotting. Called many plotting modules. sampleselect - provides UI element selecting columns matrix based sample name group. Called selectmatrix module. geneselect - provides UI element selecing rows matrix based criteria variance. Called selectmatrix module. genesets - provides UI element selecting gene sets. Called geneselect module user chooses filter gene set. plotdownload - provides download button non-Plotly plots (Plotly-driven plots export button) … smaller modules used utility functions drop-specifying various plots color based sample group. example heatmap uses selectmatrix provide UI controls subselect supplied matrices well code reads output controls actually derive subsetted matrix. Shiny modules make recycling code much, much simpler otherwise. Many can called individually, example make app dendrograms :
code: app <- prepareApp('dendro', eselist) shiny::shinyApp(ui = app$ui, server = app$server)

what: Guess whether fold change values are on a log2 or linear scale — guess_foldchange_scale
title: Guess whether fold change values are on a log2 or linear scale — guess_foldchange_scale
text: Shinyngs stores fold changes internally signed linear values (ratio two conditions, absolute magnitude least 1 sign indicating direction - see foldChange), value strictly -1 1 possible log2 scale (e.g. log2 fold change 0.5 ~1.4-fold change). log2 fold changes also typically small symmetric around zero, linear fold changes can large magnitudes effect size (e.g. 1000-fold change log2 fold change ~10).

title: Guess whether fold change values are on a log2 or linear scale — guess_foldchange_scale
text: values Numeric vector fold change values (NAs non-finite values ignored). log2_magnitude_limit Values absolute magnitude greater considered implausible log2 fold change (default 15, corresponding ~32,000-fold change).

title: Guess whether fold change values are on a log2 or linear scale — guess_foldchange_scale
text: One "log2", "linear" "ambiguous".

title: Guess whether fold change values are on a log2 or linear scale — guess_foldchange_scale
code: guess_foldchange_scale(c(-2.1, 0.3, 1.8, -0.05)) #> [1] "log2" guess_foldchange_scale(c(-8, 2, 45, -120)) #> [1] "linear" guess_foldchange_scale(c(-2, 3, 5, -8)) #> [1] "ambiguous"

title: Make UI and server functions for Shiny apps based on data supplied as modfied SummarizedExperiments — prepareApp
code: library(shinyngs)  # 1: BASIC RNA-SEQ APP  # Get an example RNA-seq dataset from the `airway` package  data(airway, package = "airway")  # Get some information about these data from the package description  expinfo <- packageDescription("airway")  # Convert to an ExploratorySummarizedExperiment (with extra slots)  ese <- as(airway, "ExploratorySummarizedExperiment")  # Make the ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList that represents the study as a # whole.  eselist <- ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList(   ese,   title = expinfo$Title,   author = expinfo$Author,   description = expinfo$Description ) #> [1] "Creating ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList object"  # Make and run the app  if (interactive()) {   app <- prepareApp("rnaseq", eselist)   shiny::shinyApp(ui = app$ui, server = app$server) }  # 2: AUGMENT WITH ANNOTATION INFO FOR MORE INFORMATIVE APP  # Use Biomart to retrieve some annotation, add it to the object, then choose a # different default grouping variable and re-make the app.  if (interactive()) {   library(biomaRt)   attributes <- c(     "ensembl_gene_id", # The sort of ID your results are keyed by     "entrezgene", # Could be used for gene sets keyed by Entrez ID     "external_gene_name" # Used to annotate gene names on the plot   )    mart <- useMart(     biomart = "ENSEMBL_MART_ENSEMBL",     dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl", host = "www.ensembl.org"   )   annotation <- getBM(attributes = attributes, mart = mart)   annotation <- annotation[order(annotation$entrezgene), ]    mcols(ese) <- annotation[match(rownames(ese), annotation$ensembl_gene_id), ]   ese@labelfield <- "external_gene_name"    eselist <- ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList(     ese,     title = expinfo$Title,     author = expinfo$Author,     description = expinfo$Description,     default_groupvar = "dex"   )   app <- prepareApp("rnaseq", eselist)   shiny::shinyApp(ui = app$ui, server = app$server) }  # 3. MORE COMPLEX DATA FOR DIFFERENTIAL EXPRESSION ETC  # See the vignette for how to populate the extra slots of an # ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList (contrasts, differential statistics, # gene set analyses etc.). With those in place the resulting app gains # additional panels for differential analyses.

Used multiple modules, including foldchangeplot volcanoplot scatterplotcontrols set controls scatter plots.
